revision.text: #redirect[[en:Kō_Shrine]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{神社| 名称=興神社| 所在地=長崎県壱岐市芦辺町湯岳興触676| 祭神=足仲彦命
息長足姫命| 社格=式内社・壱岐国一宮・村社| 例祭=[[4月13日]]}} '''興神社'''（こうじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]、[[壱岐国]][[一宮]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 足仲彦命と息長足姫命（[[神功皇后]]）を祀る。 附近にはかつて壱岐[[国府]]があったと考えられている。社名の「興」は「国府（こふ、こう）」の意味である。国府の倉の鍵である「印鑰（いんにゃく）」を保管していたことから、近世には「印鑰神社」とも呼ばれていた。境内の壱岐総社神社は壱岐国の[[総社]]である。 [[延宝]]4年（[[1676年]]）、神道家[[橘三喜]]が壱岐の式内社を調査した際に、当社を式内小社「與神社」に比定した。しかし、これは興と與（与）を見誤ったためと考えられており、近年の研究では、式内[[名神大社]]の[[天手長男神社]]が当社であり、壱岐国一宮であったとする説が有力となっている。延宝4年の調査の際に、天手長男神社は当社とは別の神社に比定され、壱岐国一宮の称もその神社に移ったが、それ以降も当社を「一の宮」と呼ぶ通称は残った。 明治9年（1876年）12月に村社に列し、明治40年7月に[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定された。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/iki/kou_title.htm 興神社]（玄松子） [[Category:長崎県の神社|こうしんしや]] [[Category:名神大社|こうしんしや]] [[Category:式内小社|こうしんしや]] [[Category:一宮|こうしんしや]] [[Category:村社|こうしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=興神社| 所在地=長崎県壱岐市芦辺町湯岳興触676| 祭神=足仲彦命
息長足姫命| 社格=式内社・壱岐国一宮・村社| 例祭=[[4月13日]]}} '''興神社'''（こうじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]、[[壱岐国]][[一宮]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 足仲彦命（[[仲哀天皇]]）と息長足姫命（[[神功皇后]]）を祀る。 附近にはかつて壱岐[[国府]]があったと考えられている。社名の「興」は「国府（こふ、こう）」の意味である。国府の倉の鍵である「印鑰（いんにゃく）」を保管していたことから、近世には「印鑰神社」とも呼ばれていた。境内の壱岐総社神社は壱岐国の[[総社]]である。 [[延宝]]4年（[[1676年]]）、神道家[[橘三喜]]が壱岐の式内社を調査した際に、当社を式内小社「與神社」に比定した。しかし、これは興と與（与）を見誤ったためと考えられており、近年の研究では、式内[[名神大社]]の[[天手長男神社]]が当社であり、壱岐国一宮であったとする説が有力となっている。延宝4年の調査の際に、天手長男神社は当社とは別の神社に比定され、壱岐国一宮の称もその神社に移ったが、それ以降も当社を「一の宮」と呼ぶ通称は残った。 明治9年（1876年）12月に村社に列し、明治40年7月に[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定された。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/iki/kou_title.htm 興神社]（玄松子） {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:壱岐市]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=興神社| 所在地=長崎県壱岐市芦辺町湯岳興触676| 祭神=足仲彦命
息長足姫命| 社格=式内社・壱岐国一宮・村社| 例祭=[[4月13日]]}} '''興神社'''（こうじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]、[[壱岐国]][[一宮]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 足仲彦命（[[仲哀天皇]]）と息長足姫命（[[神功皇后]]）を祀る。 附近にはかつて壱岐[[国府]]があったと考えられている。社名の「興」は「国府（こふ、こう）」の意味である。国府の倉の鍵である「印鑰（いんにゃく）」を保管していたことから、近世には「印鑰神社」とも呼ばれていた。境内の壱岐総社神社は壱岐国の[[総社]]である。 [[延宝]]4年（[[1676年]]）、神道家[[橘三喜]]が壱岐の式内社を調査した際に、当社を式内小社「與神社」に比定した。しかし、これは興と與（与）を見誤ったためと考えられており、近年の研究では、式内[[名神大社]]の[[天手長男神社]]が当社であり、壱岐国一宮であったとする説が有力となっている。延宝4年の調査の際に、天手長男神社は当社とは別の神社に比定され、壱岐国一宮の称もその神社に移ったが、それ以降も当社を「一の宮」と呼ぶ通称は残った。 明治9年（1876年）12月に村社に列し、明治40年7月に[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定された。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/iki/kou_title.htm 興神社]（玄松子） {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:壱岐市]] [[Category:壱岐国]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 興神社 |画像 = [[画像:Gazoubosyu.jpg]] |所在地 = 長崎県壱岐市芦辺町湯岳興触676 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|46|11.3|N|129|43|54.2|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 足仲彦命・息長足姫命 |社格 = 式内大社（名神大）・壱岐国一宮・旧村社 |創建 = |本殿 = [[流造|一間社流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = 壱岐国大七社 |例祭 = 4月13日 |神事 = }}
'''興神社'''（こうじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]、[[壱岐国]][[一宮]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。
== 祭神 == 足仲彦命（[[仲哀天皇]]）と息長足姫命（[[神功皇后]]）を祀る。
== 由緒 == 附近にはかつて壱岐[[国府]]があったと考えられている。社名の「興」は「国府（こふ、こう）」の意味である。国府の倉の鍵である「印鑰（いんにゃく）」を保管していたことから、近世には「印鑰神社」とも呼ばれていた。境内の壱岐総社神社は壱岐国の[[総社]]である。
[[延宝]]4年（[[1676年]]）、神道家[[橘三喜]]が壱岐の式内社を調査した際に、当社を式内小社「與神社」に比定した。しかし、これは興と與（与）を見誤ったためと考えられており、近年の研究では、式内[[名神大社]]の「天手長男神社」が当社であり、壱岐国一宮であったとする説が有力となっている。延宝4年の調査の際に、天手長男神社は[[郷ノ浦町]]田中触の若宮社に比定され、壱岐国一宮の称もその神社に移ったが（現[[天手長男神社]]）、それ以降も当社を「一の宮」と呼ぶ通称は残った。
[[明治]]9年（1876年）12月に村社に列し、明治40年7月に[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定された。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.genbu.net/data/iki/kou_title.htm 興神社]（玄松子「玄松子の記憶」（個人サイト））
{{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや}} [[Category:壱岐市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:式内小社]]<!-- 両方入っているのは、上のような事情があります。誤記ではありません。 --> [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:長崎県の旧村社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 興神社 |画像 = [[画像:Gazoubosyu.jpg]] |所在地 = 長崎県壱岐市芦辺町湯岳興触676 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|46|11.3|N|129|43|54.2|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 足仲彦命・息長足姫命 |社格 = 式内大社（名神大）・壱岐国一宮・旧村社 |創建 = |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = 壱岐国大七社 |例祭 = 4月13日 |神事 = }}
'''興神社'''（こうじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]、[[壱岐国]][[一宮]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は村社。
== 祭神 == 足仲彦命（[[仲哀天皇]]）と息長足姫命（[[神功皇后]]）を祀る。
== 由緒 == 附近にはかつて壱岐[[国府]]があったと考えられている。社名の「興」は「国府（こふ、こう）」の意味である。国府の倉の鍵である「印鑰（いんにゃく）」を保管していたことから、近世には「印鑰神社」とも呼ばれていた。境内の壱岐総社神社は壱岐国の[[総社]]である。
[[延宝]]4年（[[1676年]]）、神道家[[橘三喜]]が壱岐の式内社を調査した際に、当社を式内小社「與神社」に比定した。しかし、これは興と與（与）を見誤ったためと考えられており、近年の研究では、式内[[名神大社]]の「[[天手長男神社]]」が当社であり、壱岐国一宮であったとする説が有力となっている。延宝4年の調査の際に、天手長男神社は壱岐市内の郷ノ浦町田中触の若宮社に比定され、壱岐国一宮の称もその神社に移ったが（現・天手長男神社）、それ以降も当社を「一の宮」と呼ぶ通称は残った。
[[明治]]9年（1876年）12月に村社に列し、明治40年7月に[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定された。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.genbu.net/data/iki/kou_title.htm 興神社]（玄松子「玄松子の記憶」（個人サイト））

拝殿 |所在地 = 長崎県壱岐市芦辺町湯岳興触676 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|46|11.3|N|129|43|54.2|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 足仲彦命・息長足姫命 |社格 = 式内大社（名神大）・壱岐国一宮・旧村社 |創建 = |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = 壱岐国大七社 |例祭 = 4月13日 |神事 = }} '''興神社'''（こうじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]、[[壱岐国]][[一宮]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は村社。 == 祭神 == 足仲彦命（[[仲哀天皇]]）と息長足姫命（[[神功皇后]]）を祀る。 == 由緒 == 附近にはかつて壱岐[[国府]]があったと考えられている。社名の「興」は「国府（こふ、こう）」の意味である。国府の倉の鍵である「印鑰（いんにゃく）」を保管していたことから、近世には「印鑰神社」とも呼ばれていた。境内の壱岐総社神社は壱岐国の[[総社]]である。 [[延宝]]4年（[[1676年]]）、神道家[[橘三喜]]が壱岐の式内社を調査した際に、当社を式内小社「與神社」に比定した。しかし、これは興と與（与）を見誤ったためと考えられており、近年の研究では、式内[[名神大社]]の「[[天手長男神社]]」が当社であり、壱岐国一宮であったとする説が有力となっている。延宝4年の調査の際に、天手長男神社は壱岐市内の郷ノ浦町田中触の若宮社に比定され、壱岐国一宮の称もその神社に移ったが（現・天手長男神社）、それ以降も当社を「一の宮」と呼ぶ通称は残った。 [[明治]]9年（1876年）12月に村社に列し、明治40年7月に[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定された。 {{commonscat|Kou-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや}} [[Category:壱岐市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:長崎県の旧村社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 興神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|280px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町湯岳興触676 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|46|11.3|N|129|43|54.2|E|region:JP|name=興神社}} |祭神 = [[仲哀天皇|足仲彦命]]
[[神功皇后|息長足姫命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）[[論社]]
[[壱岐国]][[一宮]]
旧[[村社]] |創建 = |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = 壱岐国大七社 |例祭 = [[4月13日]] |神事 = }} '''興神社'''（こうじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）[[論社]]、[[壱岐国]][[一宮]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == * '''足仲彦命''' （[[仲哀天皇]]） * '''息長足姫命''' （[[神功皇后]]） == 歴史 == 付近にはかつて壱岐[[国府]]があったと考えられており、社名の「興」は「国府（こふ、こう）」の意味とされる。国府の倉の鍵である「印鑰（いんにゃく）」を保管していたことから、近世には「印鑰神社」とも呼ばれていた。境内の壱岐総社神社は壱岐国の[[総社]]にあたる。 [[延宝]]4年（[[1676年]]）、神道家[[橘三喜]]が壱岐の式内社を調査した際に、当社を式内小社「與神社」に比定した。しかし、これは興と與（与）を見誤ったためと考えられており、近年の研究では、式内[[名神大社]]の「[[天手長男神社]]」が当社であり、壱岐国一宮であったとする説が有力となっている。延宝4年の調査の際に、天手長男神社は壱岐市内の郷ノ浦町田中触の若宮社に比定され、壱岐国一宮の称もその神社に移ったが（現 [[天手長男神社]]）、それ以降も当社を「一の宮」と呼ぶ通称は残った。 [[明治]]9年（1876年）12月に村社に列し、明治40年7月に[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定された。 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町湯岳興触676 == 関連項目 == * [[天手長男神社]] - 壱岐一宮を称するもう1つの神社 == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Kou-jinja}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/676002.html 天手長男神社]（國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや}} [[Category:壱岐市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:長崎県の旧村社]] [[Category:壱岐国|社こうしんしや]]

旧[[村社]] |創建 = |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = 壱岐国大七社 |例祭 = [[4月13日]] |神事 = }} '''興神社'''（こうじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）[[論社]]、[[壱岐国]][[一宮]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == * '''足仲彦命''' （[[仲哀天皇]]） * '''息長足姫命''' （[[神功皇后]]） == 歴史 == 付近にはかつて壱岐[[国府]]があったと考えられており、社名の「興」は「国府（こふ、こう）」の意味とされる。国府の倉の鍵である「印鑰（いんにゃく）」を保管していたことから、近世には「印鑰神社」とも呼ばれていた。境内の壱岐総社神社は壱岐国の[[総社]]にあたる。 [[延宝]]4年（[[1676年]]）、神道家[[橘三喜]]が壱岐の式内社を調査した際に、当社を式内小社「與神社」に比定した。しかし、これは興と與（与）を見誤ったためと考えられており、近年の研究では、式内[[名神大社]]の「[[天手長男神社]]」が当社であり、壱岐国一宮であったとする説が有力となっている。延宝4年の調査の際に、天手長男神社は壱岐市内の郷ノ浦町田中触の若宮社に比定され、壱岐国一宮の称もその神社に移ったが（現 [[天手長男神社]]）、それ以降も当社を「一の宮」と呼ぶ通称は残った。 [[明治]]9年（1876年）12月に村社に列し、明治40年7月に[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定された。 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町湯岳興触676 == 関連項目 == * [[天手長男神社]] - 壱岐一宮を称するもう1つの神社 * [[新城神社]] * [[壱岐神社]] == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Kou-jinja}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/676002.html 天手長男神社]（國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐国の式内社|名こうしんしや]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:長崎県の旧村社]] [[Category:壱岐国|社1]]

旧[[村社]] |創建 = |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = 壱岐国大七社 |例祭 = [[4月13日]] |神事 = |地図 = Japan Nagasaki }} '''興神社'''（こうじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町湯岳興触にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）[[論社]]、[[壱岐国]][[一宮]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の2柱。 * 足仲彦命（[[仲哀天皇]]） * 息長足姫命（[[神功皇后]]） == 歴史 == 付近にはかつて壱岐[[国府]]があったと考えられており、社名の「興」は「国府（こふ、こう）」の意味とされる。国府の倉の鍵である「印鑰（いんにゃく）」を保管していたことから、近世には「印鑰神社」とも呼ばれていた。境外社の壱岐総社神社は壱岐国の[[総社]]にあたる。 [[延宝]]4年（[[1676年]]）、神道家[[橘三喜]]が壱岐の式内社を調査した際に、当社を式内小社「與神社」に比定した。しかし、これは興と與（与）を見誤ったためと考えられており、近年の研究では、式内[[名神大社]]の「[[天手長男神社]]」が当社であり、壱岐国一宮であったとする説が有力となっている。延宝4年の調査の際に、天手長男神社は壱岐市内の郷ノ浦町田中触の若宮社に比定され、壱岐国一宮の称もその神社に移ったが（現 [[天手長男神社]]）、それ以降も当社を「一の宮」と呼ぶ通称は残った。 [[明治]]9年（1876年）12月に村社に列し、明治40年7月に[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定された。 == ギャラリー == File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|本殿 File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|参道と鳥居 File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|鳥居 File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|総社神社（壱岐国総社） == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Kou-jinja}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/676002.html 天手長男神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐国の式内社|名こうしんしや]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:長崎県の旧村社]] [[Category:壱岐国|社1]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kou-jinja_(Iki),_haiden.jpg | coordinates = https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE¶ms=33_46_11.3_N_129_43_54.2_E_ | deity = [[Emperor Chuai]],
[[Empress Jingu]],
all the deities of its province | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] }} {{draft}} {{ill|Kō Shrine|ja|興神社}} (興神社[[ja:興神社]]) is a [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] in [[Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki City]], Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to [[Harunotsuji Site]].{{Cite web |date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14 }} Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }} Kou Shrine is near the [[Harunotsuji Site|Harunotsuji Ruins]]. These ruins were once the royal capital of [[Ikikoku]]. The shrine is known as Ichinomiya. It is considered the main shrine of Iki Kuni. == Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Powers and Blessings == The shrine is known for safe childbirth and childcare. == History == The shrine was established in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It might be the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==See Also== * [[Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] * {{ill|Amanotanagao Shrine|ja|天手長男神社|simple}} ([[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) * [[Kō of Wa|Kō of Wa]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Iki Island]] [[Category:Draft articles]] }} {{shinto shrines}} [[en:draft:Kō Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kou-jinja_(Iki),_haiden.jpg | coordinates = https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE¶ms=33_46_11.3_N_129_43_54.2_E_ | deity = [[Emperor Chuai]],
[[Empress Jingu]],
all the deities of its province | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] }} {{draft}} {{ill|Kō Shrine|ja|興神社}} (興神社[[ja:興神社]]) is a [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] in [[Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki City]], Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}.{{Cite web |date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14 }} [[ja:興神社]] Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }} Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Ikikoku|en}}. The shrine is known as Ichinomiya. It is considered the main shrine of Iki Kuni. == Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is known for safe childbirth and childcare.{{Clarify}} == History == The shrine was established in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==See Also== * [[Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] * {{ill|Amanotanagao Shrine|ja|天手長男神社|simple}} ([[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) * {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Iki_Gokoku_Shrine|en:draft}} {{ill|Amanotanagao Shrine|ja|天手長男神社|simple}} ([[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]] [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) * {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|4=ja|5=興}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Iki Island]] [[Category:Draft articles]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Soja shrines]] }} {{shinto shrines}} [[en:draft:Kō Shrine]]

all the deities of its province | type = [[Sōja shrine]] }} {{draft}} {{ill|Kō Shrine|ja|興神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kō_Shrine|en:draft}} (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}.{{Cite web |date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14 }} [[ja:興神社]] Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }} Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Ikikoku|en}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[Iki Province]]. Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en}} which is a kind of old government office.{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com}} It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|4=ja|5=興}} (興). == Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) ==See Also== * [[Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] * {{ill|Amanotanagao Shrine|ja|天手長男神社|simple}} ([[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) * {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Iki_Gokoku_Shrine|en:draft}} ([[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]] [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) * {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|4=ja|5=興}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Iki Island]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Soja shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] }} {{shinto shrines}} {{User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrines}}

It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) ==See Also== * [[Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] * {{ill|Amanotanagao Shrine|ja|天手長男神社|simple}} ([[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) * [[Iki Gokoku Shrine]] ([[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]] [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) * [[Kō of Wa]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Iki Island]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Soja shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] }} {{shinto shrines}} {{User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrines}}

{{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kou-jinja_(Iki),_haiden.jpg |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{coordinates|33|46|11.3|N|129|43|54.2|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Emperor Chūai]],
[[Empress Jingū]],
all the deities of its province | type = [[Sōja shrine]] }} {{draft}} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] on [[Iki Island]], Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to [[Harunotsuji Site]].{{Cite web |date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14 }} Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }} [[ja:興神社]] Kou Shrine is near the [[Harunotsuji Site]]. These ruins were once the royal capital of [[Iki Island|Ikikoku]]. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[Iki Province]]. Its name comes from [[Kokufu]] which is a kind of old government office.{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com}} It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan [[Kō of Wa]] (興). == Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) ==See Also== * [[Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] * {{ill|Amanotanagao Shrine|ja|天手長男神社|simple}} ([[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) * [[Iki Gokoku Shrine]] ([[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]] [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) * [[Kō of Wa]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Iki Island]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Soja shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] }} {{shinto shrines}} {{User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrines}}

These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. {{Expand French|date=2024年4月}} {{神社 |名称 = 興神社 |画像 = [[File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町湯岳興触676 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|46|11.3|N|129|43|54.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[仲哀天皇|足仲彦命]]
[[神功皇后|息長足姫命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）[[論社]]
[[壱岐国]][[一宮]]
旧[[村社]] |創建 = |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = 壱岐国大七社 |例祭 = [[4月13日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=興神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''興神社'''（こうじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町湯岳興触にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）[[論社]]、[[壱岐国]][[一宮]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の2柱。 * 足仲彦命（[[仲哀天皇]]） * 息長足姫命（[[神功皇后]]） == 歴史 == 付近にはかつて壱岐[[国府]]があったと考えられており、社名の「興」は「国府（こふ、こう）」の意味とされる。国府の倉の鍵である「印鑰（いんにゃく）」を保管していたことから、近世には「印鑰神社」とも呼ばれていた。境外社の壱岐総社神社は壱岐国の[[総社]]にあたる。 [[延宝]]4年（[[1676年]]）、神道家[[橘三喜]]が壱岐の式内社を調査した際に、当社を式内小社「與神社」に比定した。しかし、これは興と與（与）を見誤ったためと考えられており、近年の研究では、式内[[名神大社]]の「[[天手長男神社]]」が当社であり、壱岐国一宮であったとする説が有力となっている。延宝4年の調査の際に、天手長男神社は壱岐市内の郷ノ浦町田中触の若宮社に比定され、壱岐国一宮の称もその神社に移ったが（現 [[天手長男神社]]）、それ以降も当社を「一の宮」と呼ぶ通称は残った。 [[明治]]9年（1876年）12月に村社に列し、明治40年7月に[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定された。 == ギャラリー == File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|本殿 File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|参道と鳥居 File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|鳥居 File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|総社神社（壱岐国総社） == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Kou-jinja}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/676002.html 天手長男神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名こうしんしや]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:長崎県の旧村社]] [[Category:壱岐国|社1]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]]

{{神社 |名称 = 興神社 |画像 = [[File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町湯岳興触676 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|46|11.3|N|129|43|54.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[仲哀天皇|足仲彦命]]
[[神功皇后|息長足姫命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）[[論社]]
[[壱岐国]][[一宮]]

Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en}} which is a kind of old government office.{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com}} It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|4=ja|5=興}} (興). == Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) ==See Also== * [[Ichinomiya]] * {{ill|Amanotanagao Shrine|ja|天手長男神社|simple}} ([[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) * [[Iki Gokoku Shrine]] ([[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]] [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) * [[Kō of Wa]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| {{DEFAULTSORT:Ko Shrine}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Iki Island]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Soja shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] }} {{shinto shrines}} {{User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrines}}

It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) ==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Amanotanagao Shrine]] ([[Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) ==References== {{reflist}} {{shinto shrines}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ko Shrine}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Soja shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Original pages]]

{{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kou-jinja_(Iki),_haiden.jpg |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{coordinates|33|46|11.3|N|129|43|54.2|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Emperor Chūai]],
[[Empress Jingū]],
all the deities of its province | type = [[Sōja shrine]] }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}.{{Cite web |date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14 }} Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }} Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Ikikoku|en}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[Iki Province]]. Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en}} which is a kind of old government office.{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com}} It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|4=ja|5=興}} (興). == Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) ==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Amanotanagao Shrine]] ([[Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) [[simple:Kō Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{shinto shrines}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ko Shrine}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Soja shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Original pages]]

[[Empress Jingū]],
all the deities of its province | type = [[Sōja shrine]] }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}.{{Cite web |date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14 }} Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }} Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Ikikoku|en}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[Iki Province]]. Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en}} which is a kind of old government office.{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com}} It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|4=ja|5=興}} (興). == Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) ==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Amanotanagao Shrine]] ([[Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) [[simple:Kō Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{shinto shrines}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ko Shrine}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:original pages]]

It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), {{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kou-jinja_(Iki),_haiden.jpg |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{coordinates|33|46|11.3|N|129|43|54.2|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Emperor Chūai]],
[[Empress Jingū]],
all the deities of its province | type = [[Sōja shrine]] }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}.{{Cite web |date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14 }} Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }} Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Ikikoku|en}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[Iki Province]]. Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en}} which is a kind of old government office.{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com}} It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|4=ja|5=興}} (興). == Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki {{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kou-jinja_(Iki),_haiden.jpg |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{coordinates|33|46|11.3|N|129|43|54.2|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Emperor Chūai]],
[[Empress Jingū]],
all the deities of its province | type = [[Sōja shrine]] }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}.{{Cite web |date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14 }} Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }} Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Ikikoku|en}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[Iki Province]]. Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en}} which is a kind of old government office.{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com}} It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|4=ja|5=興}} (興). == Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) ==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Amanotanagao Shrine]] ([[Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) [[simple:Kō Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) ==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Amanotanagao Shrine]] ([[Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) [[simple:Kō {{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kou-jinja_(Iki),_haiden.jpg |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{coordinates|33|46|11.3|N|129|43|54.2|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Emperor Chūai]],
[[Empress Jingū]],
all the deities of its province | type = [[Sōja shrine]] }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}.{{Cite web |date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14 }} Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }} Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Ikikoku|en}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[Iki Province]]. Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en}} which is a kind of old government office.{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com}} It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|4=ja|5=興}} (興). == Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) ==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Amanotanagao Shrine]] ([[Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) [[simple:Kō Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{shinto shrines}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ko Shrine}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kou-jinja_(Iki),_haiden.jpg |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{coordinates|33|46|11.3|N|129|43|54.2|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Emperor Chūai]],
[[Empress Jingū]],
all the deities of its province | type = [[Sōja shrine]] }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}.{{Cite web |date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14 }} Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }} Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Ikikoku|en}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[Iki Province]]. Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en}} which is a kind of old government office.{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com}} It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|4=ja|5=興}} (興). == Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) ==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Amanotanagao Shrine]] ([[Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) [[simple:Kō Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{shinto shrines}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ko Shrine}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja all the deities of its province | type = [[Sōja shrine]] }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}.{{Cite web |date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14 }} Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }} Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Ikikoku|en}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[Iki Province]]. Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en}} which is a kind of old government office.{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com}} It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|4=ja|5=興}} (興). == Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." [[Empress Jingū]],
all the deities of its province | type = [[Sōja shrine]] }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}.{{Cite web |date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14 }} Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }} Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}. Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en}} which is a kind of old government office.{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. {{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kou-jinja_(Iki),_haiden.jpg |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{coordinates|33|46|11.3|N|129|43|54.2|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Emperor Chūai]],
[[Empress Jingū]],
all the deities of its province | type = [[Sōja shrine]] }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}.{{Cite web |date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14 }} Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }} Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Ikikoku|en}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[Iki Province]]. Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en}} which is a kind of old government office.{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com}} It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|4=ja|5=興}} (興). == Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) ==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Amanotanagao Shrine]] ([[Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) [[simple:Kō Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{shinto shrines}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ko Shrine}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]] {{Sōja shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kō Shrine-qq]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Kō Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Kō Shrine]] [[Category:Qq]]{{Iki Shrines}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:興神社]] [[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]]

{{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kou-jinja_(Iki),_haiden.jpg |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{coordinates|33|46|11.3|N|129|43|54.2|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Emperor Chūai]],
[[Empress Jingū]],
all the deities of its province | type = [[Sōja shrine]] }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}.{{Cite web |date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14 }} Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }} Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Ikikoku|en}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[Iki Province]]. Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en}} which is a kind of old government office.{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com}} It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|4=ja|5=興}} (興). == Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) ==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Amanotanagao Shrine]] ([[Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) [[simple:Kō Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{shinto shrines}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ko Shrine}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]] {{Sōja shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 4]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kō Shrine-qq]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Kō Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Kō Shrine]] [[Category:Qq]]{{Iki Shrines}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[ja:Kō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:興神社]] [[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]]

It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) ==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Amanotanagao Shrine]] ([[Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) [[simple:Kō Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{shinto shrines}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ko Shrine}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]] {{Sōja shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:Tier 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Kō Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Kō Shrine]] [[Category:Qq]]{{Iki Shrines}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:興神社]] [[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]]

{{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kou-jinja_(Iki),_haiden.jpg |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{coordinates|33|46|11.3|N|129|43|54.2|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Emperor Chūai]],
[[Empress Jingū]],
all the deities of its province | type = [[Sōja shrine]] }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}.{{Cite web |date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14 }} Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }} Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Ikikoku|en}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[Iki Province]]. Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en}} which is a kind of old government office.{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com}} It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|4=ja|5=興}} (興). == Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) ==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Amanotanagao Shrine]] ([[Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) [[simple:Kō Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{shinto shrines}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ko Shrine}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]] {{Sōja shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 4]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Kō Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Kō Shrine]] [[Category:Qq]]{{Iki Shrines}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:興神社]] [[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]]

{{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kou-jinja_(Iki),_haiden.jpg |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{coordinates|33|46|11.3|N|129|43|54.2|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Emperor Chūai]],
[[Empress Jingū]],
all the deities of its province | type = [[Sōja shrine]] }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}.{{Cite web |date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14 }} Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }} Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Ikikoku|en}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[Iki Province]]. Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en}} which is a kind of old government office.{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com}} It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|4=ja|5=興}} (興). == Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) ==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Amanotanagao Shrine]] ([[Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) [[simple:Kō Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{shinto shrines}} {{citation needed|Ko Shrine}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]] {{Sōja shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:tier 4]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:11 deprecated open]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Kō Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Kō Shrine]] [[Category:Qq]]{{Iki Shrines}}[[Category:11]] [[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:興神社]] [[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]]

It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) ==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Amanotanagao Shrine]] ([[Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) [[simple:Kō Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{shinto shrines}} {{citation needed|Ko Shrine}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]] {{Sōja shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:tier 4]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:11 deprecated open]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Kō Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Kō Shrine]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]] {{Iki Shrines}}[[Category:11]] [[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:興神社]] [[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]]

{{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kou-jinja_(Iki),_haiden.jpg |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{coordinates|33|46|11.3|N|129|43|54.2|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Emperor Chūai]],
[[Empress Jingū]],
all the deities of its province | type = [[Sōja shrine]] }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}.{{Cite web |date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14 }} Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }} Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Ikikoku|en}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[Iki Province]]. Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en}} which is a kind of old government office.{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com}} It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|4=ja|5=興}} (興). == Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) ==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Amanotanagao Shrine]] ([[Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) [[simple:Kō Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{shinto shrines}} {{citation needed|Ko Shrine}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]] {{Sōja shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:tier 4]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:11 deprecated open]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Kō Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Kō Shrine]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]] {{Iki Shrines}}[[Category:11]] [[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:興神社]] [[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]]
