It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) ==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Amanotanagao Shrine]] ([[Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) [[simple:Kō Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{shinto shrines}} {{citation needed|Ko Shrine}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]] {{Sōja shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:tier 4]][[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Kō Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Kō Shrine]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]] {{Iki Shrines}}[[Category:11]] [[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:興神社]] [[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kou-jinja_(Iki),_haiden.jpg |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{coordinates|33|46|11.3|N|129|43|54.2|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Emperor Chūai]],
[[Empress Jingū]],
all the deities of its province | type = [[Sōja shrine]] }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}.{{Cite web |date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14 }} Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }} Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Ikikoku|en}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[Iki Province]]. Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en}} which is a kind of old government office.{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com}} It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|4=ja|5=興}} (興). == Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) ==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Amanotanagao Shrine]] ([[Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) [[simple:Kō Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{shinto shrines}} {{citation needed|Ko Shrine}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]] {{Sōja shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:tier 4]][[Category: Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Kō Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Kō Shrine]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]] {{Iki Shrines}}[[Category:11]] [[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:興神社]] [[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kou-jinja_(Iki),_haiden.jpg |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{coordinates|33|46|11.3|N|129|43|54.2|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Emperor Chūai]],
[[Empress Jingū]],
all the deities of its province | type = [[Sōja shrine]] }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City|12=draft}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}.{{Cite web|date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14|12=draft}} Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }} Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Ikikoku|en|12=draft}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[Iki Province]]. Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en}} which is a kind of old government office.{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com|12=draft}} It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|4=ja|5=興|12=draft}} (興). == Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." {{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kou-jinja_(Iki),_haiden.jpg |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{coordinates|33|46|11.3|N|129|43|54.2|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Emperor Chūai]],
[[Empress Jingū]],
all the deities of its province | type = [[Sōja shrine]] }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}.{{Cite web|date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14}} Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }} Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Ikikoku|en}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[Iki Province]]. Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en}} which is a kind of old government office.{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com}} It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|4=ja|5=興}} (興). == Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) ==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Amanotanagao Shrine]] ([[Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) [[simple:Kō Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{shinto shrines}} {{citation needed|Ko Shrine}} {{Iki Shrines}} {{draft categories| }} {{Sōja shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kō Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kō Shrine]] [[sn:ō Shrine]] [[ja:興神社]] [[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]]

revision.comment: 27 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:興神社 up to rev 100048405
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 興神社 |画像 = [[File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町湯岳興触676 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|46|11.3|N|129|43|54.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[仲哀天皇|足仲彦命]]
[[神功皇后|息長足姫命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）[[論社]]
[[壱岐国]][[一宮]]
旧[[村社]] |創建 = |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = 壱岐国大七社 |例祭 = [[4月13日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=興神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''興神社'''（こうじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町湯岳興触にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）[[論社]]、[[壱岐国]][[一宮]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の2柱。 * 足仲彦命（[[仲哀天皇]]） * 息長足姫命（[[神功皇后]]） == 歴史 == 付近にはかつて壱岐[[国府]]があったと考えられており、社名の「興」は「国府（こふ、こう）」の意味とされる。国府の倉の鍵である「印鑰（いんにゃく）」を保管していたことから、近世には「印鑰神社」とも呼ばれていた。境外社の壱岐総社神社は壱岐国の[[総社]]にあたる。 [[延宝]]4年（[[1676年]]）、神道家[[橘三喜]]が壱岐の式内社を調査した際に、当社を式内小社「與神社」に比定した。しかし、これは興と與（与）を見誤ったためと考えられており、近年の研究では、式内[[名神大社]]の「[[天手長男神社]]」が当社であり、壱岐国一宮であったとする説が有力となっている。延宝4年の調査の際に、天手長男神社は壱岐市内の郷ノ浦町田中触の若宮社に比定され、壱岐国一宮の称もその神社に移ったが（現 [[天手長男神社]]）、それ以降も当社を「一の宮」と呼ぶ通称は残った。 [[明治]]9年（1876年）12月に村社に列し、明治40年7月に[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定された。 == ギャラリー == File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|本殿 File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|参道と鳥居 File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|鳥居 File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|総社神社（壱岐国総社） == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Kou-jinja}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/676002.html 天手長男神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名こうしんしや]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:長崎県の旧村社]] [[Category:壱岐国|社1]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]]

[[神功皇后|息長足姫命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）[[論社]]

It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." [[Empress Jingū]],
all the deities of its province | type = [[Sōja shrine]] }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City|12=draft}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}.{{Cite web|date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14|12=draft}} Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }} Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en}}. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}.{{Cite web|date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14|qq=draft}} Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }} Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Iki Island|lt=Iki Island|lt=Ikikoku|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikikoku|qq=}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[Iki Province]]. Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu|qq=}} which is a kind of old government office.{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com|qq=draft}} It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|4=ja|5=興|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō of Wa|qq=}} (興). == Deities == The shrine honors several gods. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu|qq=}} which is a kind of old government office.{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com|qq=draft}} It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan [[:en:Kō of Wa|Kō of Wa]] (興). == Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) ==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Amanotanagao Shrine]] ([[Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) ==References== {{reflist}} {{shinto shrines}} {{Iki Shrines}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|興神社|version=100048405|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[simple:Kō Shrine]] [[sn:Kō Shrine]] [[sn:ō Shrine]] [[ja:興神社]] [[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:壱岐国]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]]

It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) ==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Amanotanagao Shrine]] ([[Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) ==References== {{reflist}} {{shinto shrines}} {{Iki Shrines}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|興神社|version=100048405|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[simple:Kō Shrine]] [[sn:Kō Shrine]] [[sn:ō Shrine]] [[ja:興神社]] [[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Iki Province]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社]] [[Category:Buildings in Iki, Nagasaki]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]]

It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) ==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Amanotanagao Shrine]] ([[Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) ==References== {{reflist}} {{shinto shrines}} {{Iki Shrines}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|興神社|version=100048405|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[simple:Kō Shrine]] [[sn:Kō Shrine]] [[sn:ō Shrine]] [[ja:興神社]] [[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Iki Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:Buildings in Iki, Nagasaki]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Commons category|Kou-jinja}} [[zh:興神社]]

{{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kou-jinja_(Iki),_haiden.jpg |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{coordinates|33|46|11.3|N|129|43|54.2|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]],
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]],
all the deities of its province | type = [[Sōja shrine]] }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki, Kagawa|qq=}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[:en:Iki Province|Iki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}.{{Cite web|date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14|qq=draft}} Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }} Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Iki Island|lt=Iki Island|lt=Ikikoku|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikikoku|qq=}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]]. Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu|qq=}} which is a kind of old government office.{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com|qq=draft}} It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan [[:en:Kō of Wa|Kō of Wa]] (興). == Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) ==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Amanotanagao Shrine]] ([[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) ==References== {{reflist}} {{shinto shrines}} {{Iki Shrines}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|興神社|version=100048405|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[simple:Kō Shrine]] [[sn:Kō Shrine]] [[sn:ō Shrine]] [[ja:興神社]] [[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Iki Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:Buildings in Iki, Nagasaki]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Commons category|Kou-jinja}} [[zh:興神社]]

Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com|qq=draft}} It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan [[:en:Kō of Wa|Kō of Wa]] (興). == Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities. It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]]. == Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors. == History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country. Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.". ==Gallery== File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) ==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Amanotanagao Shrine]] ([[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]]) ==References== {{reflist}} {{shinto shrines}} {{Iki Shrines}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|興神社|version=100048405|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[simple:Kō Shrine]] [[sn:Kō Shrine]] [[sn:ō Shrine]] [[ja:興神社]] [[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Iki Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:Buildings in Iki, Nagasaki]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[sn:Kō Shrine]] {{Commons category|Kou-jinja}} [[zh:興神社]]

It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[:en:Iki Province|Iki]] [[:en:Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a">{{Cite web|date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14|qq=draft}}</ref>
Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }}</ref>
Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Iki Island|lt=Iki Island|lt=Ikikoku|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikikoku|qq=}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu|qq=}} which is a kind of old government office.<ref name="www.viewiki.com">{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com|qq=draft}}</ref> It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan [[:en:Kō of Wa|Kō of Wa]] (興).
== Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a" />
== Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" /><ref name="www.viewiki.com" />
== History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.".<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kou-jinja]][[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]][[ja:興神社]][[simple:Kō Shrine]][[zh:興神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11613437]] Wikidata name: Kō Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{Coord|33.769806|N|129.731722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Chūai|WD=Q179971}} | type = [[Sōja shrine]] | prefecture= {{ill|Iki|WD=Q861519}} }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki, Kagawa|qq=}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[:en:Iki Province|Iki]] [[:en:Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a">{{Cite web|date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14|qq=draft}}</ref>
Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }}</ref>
Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Iki Island|lt=Iki Island|lt=Ikikoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikikoku|qq=}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu|qq=}} which is a kind of old government office.<ref name="www.viewiki.com">{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com|qq=draft}}</ref> It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan [[:en:Kō of Wa|Kō of Wa]] (興).
== Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a" />
== Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" /><ref name="www.viewiki.com" />
== History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.".<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />

[[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Kō Shrine]]
[[ja:興神社]] [[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]]
<!--Kō Shrine--> [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Iki Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:Buildings in Iki, Nagasaki]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]]
<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Kou-jinja}} [[zh:興神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11613437}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kou-jinja]][[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]][[ja:興神社]][[simple:Kō Shrine]][[zh:興神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11613437]] Wikidata name: Kō Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{Coord|33.769806|N|129.731722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Chūai|WD=Q179971}} | type = [[Sōja shrine]] | prefecture= {{ill|Iki|WD=Q861519}} }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki, Kagawa|qq=}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[:en:Iki Province|Iki]] [[:en:Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a">{{Cite web|date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14|qq=draft}}</ref>
Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }}</ref>
Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Iki Island|lt=Iki Island|lt=Ikikoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikikoku|qq=}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu|qq=}} which is a kind of old government office.<ref name="www.viewiki.com">{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com|qq=draft}}</ref> It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan [[:en:Kō of Wa|Kō of Wa]] (興).
== Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a" />
== Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" /><ref name="www.viewiki.com" />
== History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.".<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) </gallery>
==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Ame-no-Tanagao Shrine]] ([[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]])

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] to [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kou-jinja]][[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]][[ja:興神社]][[simple:Kō Shrine]][[zh:興神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11613437]] Wikidata name: Kō Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{Coord|33.769806|N|129.731722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Chūai|WD=Q179971}} | type = [[Sōja shrine]] | prefecture= {{ill|Iki|WD=Q861519}} }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki, Kagawa|qq=}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[:en:Iki Province|Iki]] [[:en:Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a">{{Cite web|date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14|qq=draft}}</ref>
Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }}</ref>
Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Iki Island|lt=Iki Island|lt=Ikikoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikikoku|qq=}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu|qq=}} which is a kind of old government office.<ref name="www.viewiki.com">{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com|qq=draft}}</ref> It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan [[:en:Kō of Wa|Kō of Wa]] (興).
== Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a" />
== Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" /><ref name="www.viewiki.com" />
== History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.".<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) </gallery>
==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Ame-no-Tanagao Shrine]] ([[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]])

These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Iki Island|lt=Iki Island|lt=Ikikoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikikoku|qq=}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />

== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==
'''Kō Shrine (Q11613437)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Iki Island]].
== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! {{wikidata link|Q11613437}}
[[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kou-jinja]][[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]][[ja:興神社]][[simple:Kō Shrine]][[zh:興神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11613437]] Wikidata name: Kō Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{Coord|33.769806|N|129.731722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Chūai|WD=Q179971}} | type = [[Sōja shrine]] | prefecture= {{ill|Iki|WD=Q861519}} }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki, Kagawa|qq=}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[:en:Iki Province|Iki]] [[:en:Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a">{{Cite web|date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14|qq=draft}}</ref>
Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }}</ref>
Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Iki Island|lt=Iki Island|lt=Ikikoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikikoku|qq=}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu|qq=}} which is a kind of old government office.<ref name="www.viewiki.com">{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com|qq=draft}}</ref> It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan [[:en:Kō of Wa|Kō of Wa]] (興).
== Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a" />
== Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" /><ref name="www.viewiki.com" />
== History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.".<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) </gallery>
==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Ame-no-Tanagao Shrine]] ([[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]])

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kou-jinja]][[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]][[ja:興神社]][[simple:Kō Shrine]][[zh:興神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11613437]] Wikidata name: Kō Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{Coord|33.769806|N|129.731722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Chūai|WD=Q179971}} | type = [[Sōja shrine]] | prefecture= {{ill|Iki|WD=Q861519}} }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki, Kagawa|qq=}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[:en:Iki Province|Iki]] [[:en:Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a">{{Cite web|date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14|qq=draft}}</ref>
Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }}</ref>
Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Iki Island|lt=Iki Island|lt=Ikikoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikikoku|qq=}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu|qq=}} which is a kind of old government office.<ref name="www.viewiki.com">{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com|qq=draft}}</ref> It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan [[:en:Kō of Wa|Kō of Wa]] (興).
== Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a" />
== Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" /><ref name="www.viewiki.com" />
== History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.".<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) </gallery>

The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />

Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com|qq=draft}}</ref> It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan [[:en:Kō of Wa|Kō of Wa]] (興).
== Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a" />
== Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" /><ref name="www.viewiki.com" />
== History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.".<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kou-jinja]][[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]][[ja:興神社]][[simple:Kō Shrine]][[zh:興神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kou-jinja]][[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]][[ja:興神社]][[simple:Kō Shrine]][[zh:興神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kou-jinja]][[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]][[ja:興神社]][[simple:Kō Shrine]][[zh:興神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11613437]] Wikidata name: Kō Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{Coord|33.769806|N|129.731722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Chūai|WD=Q179971}} | type = [[Sōja shrine]] | prefecture= {{ill|Iki|WD=Q861519}} }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki, Kagawa|qq=}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[:en:Iki Province|Iki]] [[:en:Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a">{{Cite web|date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14|qq=draft}}</ref>
Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }}</ref>
Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Iki Island|lt=Iki Island|lt=Ikikoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikikoku|qq=}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu|qq=}} which is a kind of old government office.<ref name="www.viewiki.com">{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com|qq=draft}}</ref> It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan [[:en:Kō of Wa|Kō of Wa]] (興).
== Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a" />
== Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" /><ref name="www.viewiki.com" />
== History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.".<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) </gallery>
==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Ame-no-Tanagao Shrine]] ([[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]])

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kou-jinja]][[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]][[ja:興神社]][[simple:Kō Shrine]][[zh:興神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kou-jinja]][[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]][[ja:興神社]][[simple:Kō Shrine]][[zh:興神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kou-jinja]][[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]][[ja:興神社]][[simple:Kō Shrine]][[zh:興神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11613437]] Wikidata name: Kō Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{Coord|33.769806|N|129.731722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Chūai|WD=Q179971}} | type = [[Sōja shrine]] | prefecture= {{ill|Iki|WD=Q861519}} }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki, Kagawa|qq=}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[:en:Iki Province|Iki]] [[:en:Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a">{{Cite web|date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14|qq=draft}}</ref>
Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }}</ref>
Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Iki Island|lt=Iki Island|lt=Ikikoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikikoku|qq=}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu|qq=}} which is a kind of old government office.<ref name="www.viewiki.com">{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com|qq=draft}}</ref> It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan [[:en:Kō of Wa|Kō of Wa]] (興).
== Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a" />
== Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" /><ref name="www.viewiki.com" />
== History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.".<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kou-jinja]][[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]][[ja:興神社]][[simple:Kō Shrine]][[zh:興神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kou-jinja]][[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]][[ja:興神社]][[simple:Kō Shrine]][[zh:興神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kou-jinja]][[fr:Sanctuaire Kō]][[ja:興神社]][[simple:Kō Shrine]][[zh:興神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11613437]] Wikidata name: Kō Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{Coord|33.769806|N|129.731722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Chūai|WD=Q179971}} | type = [[Sōja shrine]] | prefecture= {{ill|Iki|WD=Q861519}} }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki, Kagawa|qq=}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[:en:Iki Province|Iki]] [[:en:Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a">{{Cite web|date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14|qq=draft}}</ref>
Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }}</ref>
Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Iki Island|lt=Iki Island|lt=Ikikoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikikoku|qq=}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu|qq=}} which is a kind of old government office.<ref name="www.viewiki.com">{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com|qq=draft}}</ref> It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan [[:en:Kō of Wa|Kō of Wa]] (興).
== Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
It also has a shrine to all the deities of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a" />
== Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" /><ref name="www.viewiki.com" />
== History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.".<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) </gallery>
==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Ame-no-Tanagao Shrine]] ([[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]] [[Ichinomiya]])

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[simple:Kō Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[simple:Kō Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[simple:Kō Shrine]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: Kō Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{Coord|33.769806|N|129.731722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Chūai|WD=Q179971}} | type = [[Sōja shrine]] | prefecture= {{ill|Iki|WD=Q861519}} }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki, Kagawa|qq=}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in {{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki}} {{ill|Provinces of Japan|en|Provinces of Japan|WD=Q860290|lt=Province}}. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a">{{Cite web|date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14|qq=draft}}</ref>
Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }}</ref>
Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Iki Island|lt=Iki Island|lt=Ikikoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikikoku|qq=}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of {{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Province}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu|qq=}} which is a kind of old government office.<ref name="www.viewiki.com">{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com|qq=draft}}</ref> It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|Kō of Wa|WD=Q28692300|lt=Kō of Wa}} (興).
== Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
It also has a shrine to all the deities of {{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Province}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a" />
== Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" /><ref name="www.viewiki.com" />
== History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.".<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) </gallery>
==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Ame-no-Tanagao Shrine]] ({{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Province}} [[Ichinomiya]])

{{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{Coord|33.769806|N|129.731722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Chūai|WD=Q179971}} | type = [[Sōja shrine]] | prefecture= {{ill|Iki|WD=Q861519}} }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki, Kagawa|qq=}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in {{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki}} {{ill|Provinces of Japan|en|Provinces of Japan|WD=Q860290|lt=Province}}. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a">{{Cite web|date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14|qq=draft}}</ref>
Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }}</ref>
Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Iki Island|lt=Iki Island|lt=Ikikoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikikoku|qq=}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of {{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Province}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu|qq=}} which is a kind of old government office.<ref name="www.viewiki.com">{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com|qq=draft}}</ref> It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|Kō of Wa|WD=Q28692300|lt=Kō of Wa}} (興).
== Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
It also has a shrine to all the deities of {{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Province}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a" />
== Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" /><ref name="www.viewiki.com" />
== History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.".<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) </gallery>
==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Ame-no-Tanagao Shrine]] ({{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Province}} [[Ichinomiya]])
