{{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{Coord|33.769806|N|129.731722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Chūai|WD=Q179971}} | type = [[Sōja shrine]] | prefecture= {{ill|Iki|WD=Q861519}} }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki, Kagawa|qq=}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in {{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki}} {{ill|Provinces of Japan|en|Provinces of Japan|WD=Q860290|lt=Province}}. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a">{{Cite web|date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14|qq=draft}}</ref>
Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }}</ref>
Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Iki Island|lt=Iki Island|lt=Ikikoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikikoku|qq=}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of {{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Province}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu|qq=}} which is a kind of old government office.<ref name="www.viewiki.com">{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com|qq=draft}}</ref> It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|Kō of Wa|WD=Q28692300|lt=Kō of Wa}} (興).
== Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
It also has a shrine to all the deities of {{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Province}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a" />
== Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" /><ref name="www.viewiki.com" />
== History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.".<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) </gallery>
==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Ame-no-Tanagao Shrine]] ({{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Province}} [[Ichinomiya]])

Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Iki Island|lt=Iki Island|lt=Ikikoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikikoku|qq=}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of {{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Province|1=Iki Province}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu|qq=}} which is a kind of old government office.<ref name="www.viewiki.com">{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com|qq=draft}}</ref> It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|Kō of Wa|WD=Q28692300|lt=Kō of Wa|1=Kō of Wa}} (興).
== Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
It also has a shrine to all the deities of {{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Province|1=Iki Province}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a" />
== Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" /><ref name="www.viewiki.com" />
== History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.".<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />

Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com|qq=draft}}</ref> It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|Kō of Wa|WD=Q28692300|lt=Kō of Wa|1=Kō of Wa}} (興).
== Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
It also has a shrine to all the deities of {{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Province|1=Iki Province}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a" />
== Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" /><ref name="www.viewiki.com" />
== History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.".<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[simple:Kō Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[simple:Kō Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[simple:Kō Shrine]]
{{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{Coord|33.769806|N|129.731722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Chūai|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}} | type = [[Sōja shrine]] | prefecture= {{ill|Iki|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}} }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki, Kagawa|qq=}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in {{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki|1=Iki Province}} {{ill|Provinces of Japan|en|Provinces of Japan|WD=Q860290|lt=Province|1=Provinces of Japan}}. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a">{{Cite web|date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14|qq=draft}}</ref>
Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }}</ref>
Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Iki Island|lt=Iki Island|lt=Ikikoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikikoku|qq=}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of {{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Province|1=Iki Province}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu|qq=}} which is a kind of old government office.<ref name="www.viewiki.com">{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com|qq=draft}}</ref> It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|Kō of Wa|WD=Q28692300|lt=Kō of Wa|1=Kō of Wa}} (興).
== Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
It also has a shrine to all the deities of {{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Province|1=Iki Province}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a" />
== Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" /><ref name="www.viewiki.com" />
== History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.".<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[simple:Kō Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[simple:Kō Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[simple:Kō Shrine]]
{{Infobox religious building |name=Kō Shrine|native_name=興神社|native_name_lang=ja| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG |map_type=Japan| coordinates = {{Coord|33.769806|N|129.731722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Chūai|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}} | type = [[Sōja shrine]] | prefecture= {{ill|Iki|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}} }} '''Kō Shrine''' (興神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|lt=Sanuki City|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki, Kagawa|qq=}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. It enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in {{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki|1=Iki Province}} {{ill|Provinces of Japan|en|Provinces of Japan|WD=Q860290|lt=Province|1=Provinces of Japan}}. It is located next to {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a">{{Cite web|date=2022-08-14 |title=興神社 (ko shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220814224208/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E8%88%88%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-ko-shrine/ |archive-date=2022-08-14|qq=draft}}</ref>
Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-11-21 |title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |access-date=2023-11-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841 |archive-date=2023-11-21 }}</ref>
Kou Shrine is near the {{ill|Harunotsuji Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harunotsuji Site|qq=}}. These ruins were once the royal capital of {{ill|Iki Island|lt=Iki Island|lt=Ikikoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikikoku|qq=}}. The shrine is sometimes considered the [[Ichinomiya]] or the most important shrine of {{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Province|1=Iki Province}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Its name comes from {{ill|Kokufu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu|qq=}} which is a kind of old government office.<ref name="www.viewiki.com">{{Cite web|title=The whole island of Iki is a power spot itself. Not only Rocky shore but also many other attractions|url=https://www.viewiki.com/en/sightseeing.html|access-date=2024-03-29|website=www.viewiki.com|qq=draft}}</ref> It now uses the character in the name of the old King of Japan {{ill|Kō of Wa|en|Kō of Wa|WD=Q28692300|lt=Kō of Wa|1=Kō of Wa}} (興).
== Deities == The shrine honors several gods. These include Tarashi Nakatsuhiko no Mikoto and Okinaga Tarashi Hime no Mikoto. Nintoku Tenno and Ameno Tajikarawo no Mikoto are also worshipped here. Yaomoikane no Kami and Sumiyoshi no Okami are other deities.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
It also has a shrine to all the deities of {{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Province|1=Iki Province}}.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2023a" />
== Alleged powers and blessings == The shrine is believed to give safe childbirth and childcare, fortune, and family safety to visitors.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" /><ref name="www.viewiki.com" />
== History == The shrine was built in the year 811. It is linked to governmental seals. It is possibly the first shrine in Sanuki Country.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
Kou Shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku." It is also in "Dainihonkoku Ichinomiya ki.".<ref name="webarchiveorg-2022a" />
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kou jinja Haiden.JPG|alt=拝殿|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), honden.jpg|alt=本殿|Honden (main shrine) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), keidai-torii.jpg|alt=境内鳥居|Keidai Torii (shrine gate within the precincts) File:Kou-jinja (Iki), torii.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii (shrine gate) File:Kou-jinja, haiden, Iki Island.jpg|alt=拝殿、壱岐島|Haiden on Iki Island (worship hall) File:Sousha-jinja (Iki), shaden.jpg|alt=社殿|Shaden (shrine building) </gallery>
==Related pages== * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Ame-no-Tanagao Shrine]] ({{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Province|1=Iki Province}} [[Ichinomiya]])
